raw_text:
2026 Flagship Smartphone Comparison: iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Samsung S26 Ultra vs Huawei Mate 80 Pro
I. Core Performance Comparison
Aspect	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra	Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max
Processor	Apple A19 Pro (3nm)	Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 for Galaxy (2nm)	Kirin 9030 / 9030 Pro
CPU Architecture	Hexa-core (2x 4.26GHz + 4x efficiency)	Octa-core (2x 4.74GHz Prime + 6x 3.62GHz performance)	Octa-core (exact clocks undisclosed)
GPU	A19 Pro GPU	Adreno 840	Kirin Maleoon architecture
RAM	12GB LPDDR5X	12GB / 16GB (512GB/1TB models)	12GB / 16GB
AnTuTu V10	~2,319,000	>3,720,000	~940,000 (HarmonyOS 6 environment)
Geekbench 6 (single)	Not disclosed	~3,818	~1,706 (effective ~1,774 after scaling)
Geekbench 6 (multi)	Not disclosed	~11,214	~5,479 (effective ~7,122 after scaling)
Manufacturing process	3nm	2nm	Not disclosed
Performance analysis:

Samsung S26 Ultra has the highest raw benchmark scores – thanks to its 2nm custom Snapdragon chip and aggressive high-frequency scheduling, it exceeds 3.72 million on AnTuTu. Its GPU short‑term burst performance is 5–12% faster than rivals, but after 4 minutes of high load it throttles.

Apple A19 Pro offers steady, refined performance – around 2.32 million on AnTuTu, strong single‑core performance as usual, and with the first‑ever VC vapor chamber, game frame stability has improved.

Huawei Kirin 9030 Pro still lags in raw performance – despite a significant generational boost (~7,122 multi‑core effective), it remains noticeably behind Apple and Samsung. Huawei frames this as entering a “performance saturation zone,” focusing on efficiency and AI balance rather than peak numbers.

II. Hardware Configuration
Aspect	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra	Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max
Display size	6.9 inches	6.9 inches	6.75" (Pro) / 6.9" (Pro Max)
Resolution	2868 × 1320	3120 × 1440	2832 × 1280 (Pro)
Refresh rate	120Hz ProMotion	120Hz	1–120Hz LTPO
Peak brightness	~3000 nits (outdoor)	Not disclosed	3000 nits (Pro)
Rear camera	Triple 48MP (main f/1.6, ultrawide f/2.2, 5x tele f/2.8)	200MP main (f/1.4) + 50MP ultrawide + 50MP 5x periscope + 10MP 3x tele	50MP RYYB main (f/1.4–f/4.0 variable) + 40MP ultrawide + 48MP macro tele (4x)
Battery capacity	~4832 mAh	5000 mAh	5750 mAh (Pro) / 6000 mAh (Pro Max)
Wired charging	~40W (50% in 20 min)	45W	100W SuperCharge
Wireless charging	25W MagSafe	Not disclosed	80W wireless
Water resistance	IP68 (6m for 30 min)	IP68	IP68
Weight	Not disclosed	~214g	Not disclosed
Hardware analysis:

Display: All three have top‑tier 120Hz LTPO OLED panels. Samsung has a slight resolution edge, while Apple and Huawei both reach 3000 nits outdoor brightness – daily use is indistinguishable.

Camera system: Samsung offers the most complete focal length coverage (200MP main + dual tele). Apple achieves uniform 48MP across all three lenses and remains the video benchmark. Huawei continues with variable aperture (f/1.4–f/4.0) plus RYYB sensor and XMAGE algorithm, delivering a distinctive color style and strong low‑light performance.

Battery & charging: Huawei leads by a large margin – 5750/6000 mAh + 100W wired is far superior to Apple’s ~4832 mAh / 40W and Samsung’s 5000 mAh / 45W. However, iPhone’s power efficiency under light loads can sometimes result in comparable or even better real‑world battery life than some larger‑battery Android phones.

III. System & User Experience
Aspect	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra	Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max
OS	iOS 26	One UI 8.5 (Android 16)	HarmonyOS 6.0 / 6.1 (Pure Harmony)
Update commitment	~5–6 years	7 years of OS & security updates	Not disclosed
Gaming performance	1h Genshin Impact max settings: ≥59 fps, max temp ~41°C	High‑load mobile games avg 54 fps, some dips	Genshin Impact max settings: stable 59 fps, good long‑session thermals
Thermal management	First VC vapor chamber + unibody aluminum shell, warm but not hot	Graphene cooling area +25%, notable improvement over previous gen	Standard model decent; Pro Max “Wind Speed” edition has active fan cooling
Smoothness	Very high, though some users report occasional iOS 26 frame drops	One UI 8.5 animations smooth, no stutter	HarmonyOS 6.0 daily smoothness greatly improved, heavy‑load frame stability +50%
Signal performance	Mixed reputation	Weak‑network stability needs refinement	Huawei’s traditional strength – Lingxi antenna technology
User experience summary:

Gamers: Apple offers the most reliable frame stability and best‑ever iPhone thermals. Huawei’s Pro Max Wind Speed edition uses active cooling to excel in sustained high‑load gaming. Samsung has the highest burst performance but throttles after ~4 minutes.

Smoothness & OS: All three are extremely smooth in daily use. iOS 26 and pure HarmonyOS 6 emphasise simplicity and fluidity (though with initial bugs); One UI 8.5 remains the most feature‑rich and customisable. Notably, Huawei Mate 80 Pro under HarmonyOS 6.0 keeps apps launching and switching smoothly even when storage is >80% full.

Signal: Huawei, drawing on its telecom heritage and Lingxi antenna technology, remains the most reliable choice for signal. iPhone’s signal reputation is middling.

IV. Special Features & Ecosystem
Feature	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra	Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max
Satellite coms	Not supported	Tiantong satellite calling (eSIM)	Pro: BeiDou messaging only; Pro Max: BeiDou + Tiantong two‑way satellite calling
AI capabilities	Apple Intelligence (fully on‑device, maximum privacy)	Galaxy AI (triple‑engine)	Pangu AI + pure HarmonyOS: sensitive tasks on‑device, general tasks optional cloud
Ecosystem synergy	Seamless Apple ecosystem (iMessage, AirDrop, Mac)	Windows / Samsung ecosystem integration	HarmonyOS Super Device, multi‑device flow, best for Chinese office & IoT ecosystem
Unique hardware	Titanium/aluminum build, 3D structured‑light Face ID	Hardware‑level privacy screen, S Pen (Ultra)	Kunlun Glass, variable‑aperture main camera, active cooling (Pro Max Wind Speed)
Biometrics / payment	Face ID + Apple Pay	Ultrasonic under‑display fingerprint + Samsung Pay	3D face payment + under‑display fingerprint + Huawei Pay
Special features analysis:

Satellite communication: Huawei Mate 80 Pro Max offers the most complete dual‑mode solution (BeiDou messaging + Tiantong calling), with ~30 free BeiDou messages per month. Samsung has also added Tiantong satellite calling. iPhone does not support satellite communication yet.

AI capabilities: All three deeply integrate AI but with different focuses: Apple emphasises on‑device privacy, Huawei uses a hybrid cloud/on‑device model, and Samsung promotes Galaxy AI for cross‑scenario intelligence. The China version of iPhone still lacks some international AI features.

Ecosystem choice: Which brand to choose heavily depends on your existing devices. Apple ecosystem gives the best closed‑loop experience for Mac, iPad, AirPods users. Huawei HarmonyOS works increasingly well with Chinese domestic office and IoT devices. Samsung suits those who prefer a mix of Windows and Android ecosystems.

V. Price Comparison (current market as of May 2026, based on 256GB starting price)
Model	Official launch price (starting)	Current discounted price	Remarks
Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	¥9999	~¥8999	¥1000 direct discount for 618, plus trade‑in offers
Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra	¥9999	~¥9999 (limited discount)	Few channel discounts; 256GB version not heavily reduced
Huawei Mate 80 Pro	¥6999 (16GB+512GB)	~¥4699–5800 (varies by channel)	Wide price fluctuation; with government subsidy as low as ~¥4700
Price analysis:

Huawei Mate 80 Pro offers outstanding value at this point – with 618 promotions and subsidies, some channels sell it for under ¥5000, over ¥4000 cheaper than similarly‑specced rivals.

Apple & Samsung both launched at ¥9999. Apple has proactively cut ¥1000 to ¥8999 for 618, while Samsung has fewer discounts due to rising memory costs. Both remain near the ¥10,000 price tier.

Watch for 618 deals – All three flagships are currently in the 618 price war. Apple and Huawei have significant subsidies; final prices may change dynamically, so compare real‑time offers before buying.

VI. Comprehensive Buying Advice
✅ Choose iPhone 17 Pro Max if you:

Are deeply invested in the Apple ecosystem (Mac, iPad, AirPods) and want seamless integration

Prioritise industry‑leading video recording and post‑production (content creator / vlogger)

Seek extreme long‑term smoothness and high resale value

Demand maximum privacy for AI (Apple Intelligence runs fully on‑device)

Don’t mind 40W charging or the lack of satellite calling

✅ Choose Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra if you:

Are a heavy mobile gamer who values GPU burst power (accepting throttling after a few minutes)

Want the absolute best display – Samsung’s calibration and sharpness are widely praised

Need the S Pen for note‑taking, drawing, or office work

Prefer Samsung/Windows ecosystem synergy and value 7 years of OS updates

Don’t care about charging speed (45W) or 5000mAh battery endurance

✅ Choose Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max if you:

Have a genuine need for satellite communication (outdoor workers, adventurers) – get the Pro Max Wind Speed edition

Are a business user who values excellent signal quality and HarmonyOS synergy with domestic office devices

Demand long battery life and fast charging (5750mAh + 100W is among the best in flagships)

Have a tighter budget but still want a flagship experience (current price advantage is huge)

Prefer pure HarmonyOS and want a locally‑optimised software and AI experience

Summary
Dimension	Winner	Reason
Raw benchmark performance	Samsung S26 Ultra	3.72M AnTuTu + 2nm chip – strongest short‑term burst
Gaming stability	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	≥59 fps + 41°C – most reliable real‑world gaming experience
Battery & charging	Huawei Mate 80 Pro	5750/6000 mAh + 100W – clear leader in specs and real‑life endurance
Video (imaging)	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Uniform 48MP + 4K 120fps – still the industry benchmark for video
Photo (versatility)	Samsung / Huawei tie	Samsung best focal coverage; Huawei unique variable aperture & color
Longest OS update support	Samsung S26 Ultra	7 years of OS & security updates – longest among flagships
Best value for money now	Huawei Mate 80 Pro	Actual price as low as ¥4699 – far cheaper than the other two
Satellite communication	Huawei Mate 80 Pro Max	Supports both BeiDou messaging + Tiantong two‑way satellite calling
One‑line buying guide:

Deep in Apple ecosystem? Take iPhone 17 Pro Max.

Crave extreme specs and burst performance? Samsung S26 Ultra.

Value signal, battery life, satellite coms, and great value? Huawei Mate 80 Pro series is currently the most compelling choice.
During the 618 sales season, keep an eye on platform subsidies and trade‑in deals to get the best price.

sections.title: Price analysis:
sections.text:
Price analysis:

Huawei Mate 80 Pro offers outstanding value at this point – with 618 promotions and subsidies, some channels sell it for under ¥5000, over ¥4000 cheaper than similarly‑specced rivals.

Apple & Samsung both launched at ¥9999. Apple has proactively cut ¥1000 to ¥8999 for 618, while Samsung has fewer discounts due to rising memory costs. Both remain near the ¥10,000 price tier.

Watch for 618 deals – All three flagships are currently in the 618 price war. Apple and Huawei have significant subsidies; final prices may change dynamically, so compare real‑time offers before buying.

VI. Comprehensive Buying Advice

sections.title: ✅ Choose iPhone 17 Pro Max if you:
sections.text:
✅ Choose iPhone 17 Pro Max if you:

Are deeply invested in the Apple ecosystem (Mac, iPad, AirPods) and want seamless integration

Prioritise industry‑leading video recording and post‑production (content creator / vlogger)

Seek extreme long‑term smoothness and high resale value

Demand maximum privacy for AI (Apple Intelligence runs fully on‑device)

Don’t mind 40W charging or the lack of satellite calling

sections.title: ✅ Choose Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra if you:
sections.text:
✅ Choose Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra if you:

Are a heavy mobile gamer who values GPU burst power (accepting throttling after a few minutes)

Want the absolute best display – Samsung’s calibration and sharpness are widely praised

Need the S Pen for note‑taking, drawing, or office work

Prefer Samsung/Windows ecosystem synergy and value 7 years of OS updates

Don’t care about charging speed (45W) or 5000mAh battery endurance

sections.title: ✅ Choose Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max if you:
sections.text:
✅ Choose Huawei Mate 80 Pro / Pro Max if you:

Have a genuine need for satellite communication (outdoor workers, adventurers) – get the Pro Max Wind Speed edition

Are a business user who values excellent signal quality and HarmonyOS synergy with domestic office devices

Demand long battery life and fast charging (5750mAh + 100W is among the best in flagships)

Have a tighter budget but still want a flagship experience (current price advantage is huge)

Prefer pure HarmonyOS and want a locally‑optimised software and AI experience

Summary
Dimension	Winner	Reason
Raw benchmark performance	Samsung S26 Ultra	3.72M AnTuTu + 2nm chip – strongest short‑term burst
Gaming stability	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	≥59 fps + 41°C – most reliable real‑world gaming experience
Battery & charging	Huawei Mate 80 Pro	5750/6000 mAh + 100W – clear leader in specs and real‑life endurance
Video (imaging)	Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max	Uniform 48MP + 4K 120fps – still the industry benchmark for video
Photo (versatility)	Samsung / Huawei tie	Samsung best focal coverage; Huawei unique variable aperture & color
Longest OS update support	Samsung S26 Ultra	7 years of OS & security updates – longest among flagships
Best value for money now	Huawei Mate 80 Pro	Actual price as low as ¥4699 – far cheaper than the other two
Satellite communication	Huawei Mate 80 Pro Max	Supports both BeiDou messaging + Tiantong two‑way satellite calling

sections.title: One‑line buying guide:
sections.text:
One‑line buying guide:

Deep in Apple ecosystem? Take iPhone 17 Pro Max.

Crave extreme specs and burst performance? Samsung S26 Ultra.

Value signal, battery life, satellite coms, and great value? Huawei Mate 80 Pro series is currently the most compelling choice.
During the 618 sales season, keep an eye on platform subsidies and trade‑in deals to get the best price.
